item.content:encoded: Aww! I really like this one. I can really sympathize with flower guy and his change from being distressed and ashamed because of the flower to accepting it has me feeling some kind of way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I quite like this. The way the character interactions were written are really nice and it's easy to sympathize with the agent. The narrative comes through well and I love it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn’t write it into the article, but he lives on-site. I imagine every SCP facility has some people who live on-site and some sites where everyone lives on-site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really enjoyed the attitude of the agent as it develops over time, but I'm a little confused about why he gets to go home, commute to work, and I guess still work at all. A dude with a flower growing out of his eye seems to me like it would tear the veil a bit, unless you're just referring to his containment unit as home.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Usually when you have a lot of extra narrative attached to a simple SCP, the whole thing comes off as a bloated mess.

This isn't one of those times, to be sure. Good stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
the agent is grumpy but I like them
eye flower is neat
+1

item.title: shit damn fellas it's a ghostchibi scp
item.content:encoded:
Music I had on loop while writing this (but has zero relevance to the SCP itself): 彗星ハネムーン/Comet Honeymoon by ナユタン星人/Nayutalien

Thanks to the following users for crit:
Osmantic (can't find your site name for some reason lol)
Dr Moned
lvdoomien
CityToast

rioludoodle mentioned that this was very kafkaesque back in ideas crit, which wasn't my intention going into it but it really does fit the definition, so I guess it's kafkaesque. Person wakes up with a flower for an eye, person deals with all the shit this causes. Mainly this is a story about how newness forces change, whether the newness is good or bad (or neutral), how that adaption is a necessary part of life, and sometimes you give a name to the adaption because it actually grows on you. Literally, in this case.

+ Show fun tidbits about this
Hide fun tidbits about this
  • This concept was originally something I thought about using for a character in a game I'm working on, but it turned into Too Many Things For One Character and ended up here instead.
  • The flower is named after the character I initially thought to assign this to. The flower is also a type of morning glory because a purple morning glory was the original flower that I'd planned to have growing from the character's eye socket.
  • The eyepatch is also (very incredibly loosely) based on an old eyepatch design of Celeste's. Man am I reusing a lot of scrapped Celeste things here.
  • This is the first time I've written a non-death and non-animal related scp.
  • This has some of my feelings on having Very Bad Eyesight and how annoying it is that everyone keeps suggesting I get contacts when I really and truly do like glasses better, and about some Mental Things that I won't go into here.
  • The original placeholder name for the agent was "Agent Blackbox" and I couldn't think of a better name for him, so he turned into Agent Daniel Shoukoku/咲鵠ダニエル (translating "blackbox" literally into Japanese, flipping the kanji, reading the kanji using the Chinese-based on-yomi readings, then assigning new kanji to the name). By sheer serendipity, one kanji I found that uses the right pronunciation included the kanji for "blooming."
